openmeter/meterexport/service.purpose: Service implementation for meter data export: reads aggregated meter rows from ClickHouse via streaming.Connector, converts them to synthetic RawEvents, and streams results via Go iterators or dual channels. Supports only SUM and COUNT aggregations; AVG/UNIQUE_COUNT are rejected at validation.

patterns.name: Config struct with validate()
patterns.description: Constructor accepts a Config; Config.validate() collects missing-field errors via errors.Join. New() returns (*service, error).
patterns.example: func New(config Config) (*service, error) { if err := config.validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }; return &service{Config: config}, nil }

patterns.name: Interface compliance assertion
patterns.description: var _ meterexport.Service = (*service)(nil) at the bottom of service.go enforces compile-time satisfaction.

patterns.name: Streaming via dual channels (resultCh + errCh)
patterns.description: Streaming methods accept chan<- T and chan<- error; both are always closed by defer in the producer. Callers must drain both before inspecting results.

patterns.name: errgroup for producer/consumer goroutines
patterns.description: ExportSyntheticMeterData spawns funnel (producer) and row-to-event conversion (consumer) via errgroup.WithContext; sync.Once guards sending ctx-cancellation errors exactly once.
patterns.example: var sendCtxErrOnce sync.Once; g, ctx := errgroup.WithContext(ctx); g.Go(producerFn); g.Go(consumerFn)

patterns.name: iter.Seq2 wrapper for channel export
patterns.description: ExportSyntheticMeterDataIter wraps the channel method in a Go 1.23 iter.Seq2[RawEvent, error], validating upfront and cancelling the child context when yield returns false.
patterns.example: seq := func(yield func(streaming.RawEvent, error) bool) { ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(ctx); defer cancel(); ... }; return seq, nil

patterns.name: Windowed pagination in funnel
patterns.description: funnel() advances queryFrom/queryTo in TARGET_ROWS_PER_QUERY (500) window steps via iterateQueryTime, checking ctx.Err() at the top of each loop.
patterns.example: const TARGET_ROWS_PER_QUERY = 500; for { if ctx.Err() != nil { errCh <- ctx.Err(); return nil }; rows, err := s.StreamingConnector.QueryMeter(ctx, ...) }

patterns.name: validateAndGetMeter reuse
patterns.description: GetTargetMeterDescriptor and ExportSyntheticMeterData both delegate to validateAndGetMeter (config validation + meter fetch + aggregation whitelist), normalising output to SUM with SUM_VALUE_PROPERTY_KEY='value'.
patterns.example: switch m.Aggregation { case meter.MeterAggregationSum, meter.MeterAggregationCount: default: return ..., fmt.Errorf("unsupported meter aggregation: %s", m.Aggregation) }

key_file_guides.role: Config struct, New() constructor, interface compliance assertion. Config is embedded in service for promoted field access.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Config embedding promotes all fields to service — avoid name collisions; update the interface assertion if meterexport.Service gains methods.

key_file_guides.role: Core export logic: validateAndGetMeter, ExportSyntheticMeterData, createEventFromMeterRow. Only SUM and COUNT produce events.
key_file_guides.watch_for: The aggregation whitelist in createEventFromMeterRow must match the switch in validateAndGetMeter; output events always use SUM_VALUE_PROPERTY_KEY='value'.

key_file_guides.role: Windowed ClickHouse query loop; funnelParams, iterateQueryTime, TARGET_ROWS_PER_QUERY.
key_file_guides.watch_for: funnel always closes resultCh and errCh via defer — callers must never close them; ctx cancellation sends ctx.Err() once (consumer dedups via sync.Once).

key_file_guides.role: iter.Seq2 wrapper starting ExportSyntheticMeterData in a goroutine, interleaving resultCh/errCh/startupErrCh via select.
key_file_guides.watch_for: startupErrCh is nilled after close to avoid repeated select hits; early yield(false) triggers ctx cancel via defer — never manually cancel before returning from yield.

key_file_guides.role: Unit tests with local MockMeterService and testutils.NewMockStreamingConnector; no DB/Ent dependency.
key_file_guides.watch_for: New aggregation types must be tested for both whitelist acceptance and rejection.

anti_patterns: Adding an aggregation type to createEventFromMeterRow without whitelisting it in validateAndGetMeter's switch — runtime rejection after validation
Closing resultCh or errCh outside funnel/ExportSyntheticMeterData — double-close panics
Skipping the sync.Once guard for ctx-cancellation errors — duplicate ctx.Err() entries on errCh
Calling ExportSyntheticMeterData synchronously without draining both channels concurrently — deadlocks when buffers fill
Adding Ent/DB dependencies — this package depends only on streaming.Connector and meter.Service interfaces

decisions.decision: Dual-channel streaming instead of returning a slice
decisions.rationale: Exports can span large time ranges; buffering all rows would OOM. Channels allow backpressure and early cancellation via context.

decisions.decision: Always normalise output to SUM aggregation (SUM_VALUE_PROPERTY_KEY='value')
decisions.rationale: Synthetic re-ingestion needs one canonical event shape; COUNT is representable as SUM=1 per occurrence while AVG/UNIQUE_COUNT cannot be safely decomposed, so they are rejected upfront.

decisions.decision: iter.Seq2 wrapper as the preferred caller API
decisions.rationale: Go 1.23 range-over-function is cleaner than manual channel management; the wrapper handles ctx cancellation on early break via deferred cancel.

code_examples.scenario: Consume exported events via the iterator API with early-break safety
